title: pilosella longisquama subsp. naretensis
intro: Pilosella longisquama subsp. naretensis, also known as the Naret's mouse-ear or Naret's dandelion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pilosella longisquama subsp. naretensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, it is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be taken internally to promote overall well-being.
